steps.title: Identify the verb-object structure
steps.content: The blank comes right after the verb compiled and right before the noun phrase climate data from several sites in Puerto Rico's Luquillo Mountains that it acts upon. There is nothing separating them—no clause, list, or other element.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
No punctuation separates a verb from what it acts upon in Standard English.

Choice B follows this by keeping compiled adjacent to climate data.

Choices A, C, and D each insert a comma, colon, or period between the verb and object, violating this convention.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
